frames: The agent lies flat on its belly with all four limbs splayed outwards, making full contact with the ground.
It begins to lift its central body slightly, bending its front two limbs inward while the rear limbs remain extended.
The agent continues to elevate its body, with all four limbs now actively supporting its weight and bending at the joints.
Its body is now significantly raised off the ground, supported by its four bent limbs in a low crouch.
The agent achieves a stable, elevated quadrupedal stance, with all four limbs firmly planted and supporting the central sphere.
It maintains this stable, elevated posture, with its limbs bent and making continuous contact with the ground.
The agent slightly adjusts its weight, but its overall quadrupedal stance and limb positions remain largely unchanged.
It holds its elevated, static quadrupedal posture, showing no forward movement or change in gait.
